# Job Description: Software Testdesigner (m/w/d)

**Company:** intersoft GmbH

**Location:** Hamburg, Germany (Domstraße 18, 20095 Hamburg)

**Employment Type:** Full-time or Part-time | Permanent Position

**Seniority Level:** Experienced (5-7 years)

---

## Responsibilities

The role involves designing tests based on business specifications and documentation creation. Key duties include:

- Estimating and planning test activities alongside business analysts and developers
- Selecting appropriate testing methodologies to ensure software quality and implement new requirements
- Supporting teams in analyzing error tickets using technical expertise
- Continuously refining test strategies and approaches to maintain existing systems
- Communicating proactively and collaboratively with colleagues at intersoft and parent company WWK

---

## Requirements

Candidates should demonstrate:

- Relevant experience in test automation and software testing, preferably in commission/provisioning environments
- Familiarity with agile methodologies and proficiency with common automation tools
- Strong technical understanding of testing methods and techniques
- Problem-solving abilities combined with assertiveness and consensus-building skills
- Detail-oriented, structured work approach with quality consciousness
- Formal training as an IT specialist, completed degree, or comparable insurance industry background with technical aptitude

---

## What They Offer

- Flexible working arrangements and remote work options
- Flat hierarchies and collaborative team environment
- Modern equipment for office and home use
- Germany rail pass coverage, company sports, continuing education programs
- On-site perks including ice flatrate, beverages, and fruit
- Work-life balance and professional development opportunities

---

## Application Contact

Candidates should submit applications to **jobs@intersoft.de** with salary expectations and earliest start date. HR contact: Miriam.

from docx import Document
from docx.shared import Pt, RGBColor, Inches, Cm
from docx.enum.text import WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH
from docx.enum.table import WD_TABLE_ALIGNMENT, WD_ALIGN_VERTICAL
from docx.oxml.ns import qn
from docx.oxml import OxmlElement
import copy

NAVY = RGBColor(0x1B, 0x2A, 0x4A)
DARK_NAVY = RGBColor(0x0D, 0x1B, 0x33)
MID_GREY = RGBColor(0x55, 0x55, 0x55)
LIGHT_GREY = RGBColor(0x88, 0x88, 0x88)
WHITE = RGBColor(0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF)
ACCENT = RGBColor(0x2E, 0x75, 0xB6)

OUTPUT_PATH = "/c/Users/nugad/Downloads/Dolapo_Nuga_PM_PV_BESS_CV_2026.docx"


def set_cell_bg(cell, hex_color):
    tc = cell._tc
    tcPr = tc.get_or_add_tcPr()
    shd = OxmlElement('w:shd')
    shd.set(qn('w:val'), 'clear')
    shd.set(qn('w:color'), 'auto')
    shd.set(qn('w:fill'), hex_color)
    tcPr.append(shd)


def add_paragraph_border_bottom(paragraph, color_hex="1B2A4A", size=12):
    pPr = paragraph._p.get_or_add_pPr()
    pBdr = OxmlElement('w:pBdr')
    bottom = OxmlElement('w:bottom')
    bottom.set(qn('w:val'), 'single')
    bottom.set(qn('w:sz'), str(size))
    bottom.set(qn('w:space'), '4')
    bottom.set(qn('w:color'), color_hex)
    pBdr.append(bottom)
    pPr.append(pBdr)


def set_paragraph_spacing(paragraph, before=0, after=0, line=None):
    pPr = paragraph._p.get_or_add_pPr()
    spacing = OxmlElement('w:spacing')
    spacing.set(qn('w:before'), str(before))
    spacing.set(qn('w:after'), str(after))
    if line:
        spacing.set(qn('w:line'), str(line))
        spacing.set(qn('w:lineRule'), 'auto')
    pPr.append(spacing)


def remove_paragraph_spacing(paragraph):
    pPr = paragraph._p.get_or_add_pPr()
    spacing = OxmlElement('w:spacing')
    spacing.set(qn('w:before'), '0')
    spacing.set(qn('w:after'), '0')
    pPr.append(spacing)


def add_section_heading(doc, text):
    p = doc.add_paragraph()
    set_paragraph_spacing(p, before=160, after=40)
    run = p.add_run(text.upper())
    run.bold = True
    run.font.size = Pt(10)
    run.font.color.rgb = NAVY
    run.font.name = 'Calibri'
    add_paragraph_border_bottom(p, "1B2A4A", 12)
    return p


def add_job_entry(doc, title, company, location, dates, bullets):
    # Title and company on same line
    p = doc.add_paragraph()
    set_paragraph_spacing(p, before=120, after=20)
    r_title = p.add_run(title)

    r_title.font.color.rgb = DARK_NAVY

    r_sep = p.add_run('  |  ')

    r_sep.font.color.rgb = LIGHT_GREY
    r_sep.font.name = 'Calibri'
    r_company = p.add_run(company)
    r_company.bold = True
    r_company.font.size = Pt(10.5)
    r_company.font.color.rgb = ACCENT

    # Location and dates line
    p2 = doc.add_paragraph()
    set_paragraph_spacing(p2, before=0, after=60)
    r_loc = p2.add_run(f"{location}   \u2022   {dates}")
    r_loc.italic = True
    r_loc.font.size = Pt(9.5)
    r_loc.font.color.rgb = LIGHT_GREY
    r_loc.font.name = 'Calibri'

    for bullet in bullets:
        pb = doc.add_paragraph(style='List Bullet')
        set_paragraph_spacing(pb, before=0, after=20)
        pb.paragraph_format.left_indent = Inches(0.2)
        pb.paragraph_format.first_line_indent = Inches(-0.15)
        r = pb.add_run(bullet)
        r.font.size = Pt(9.5)
        r.font.color.rgb = MID_GREY
        r.font.name = 'Calibri'

def set_cell_bg(cell, hex_color):
    tc = cell._tc
    tcPr = tc.get_or_add_tcPr()
    shd = OxmlElement('w:shd')
    shd.set(qn('w:val'), 'clear')
    shd.set(qn('w:color'), 'auto')
    shd.set(qn('w:fill'), hex_color)
    tcPr.append(shd)

    pPr = paragraph._p.get_or_add_pPr()
    pBdr = OxmlElement('w:pBdr')
    bottom = OxmlElement('w:bottom')
    bottom.set(qn('w:val'), 'single')
    bottom.set(qn('w:sz'), str(size))
    bottom.set(qn('w:space'), '4')
    bottom.set(qn('w:color'), color_hex)
    pBdr.append(bottom)
    pPr.append(pBdr)

    pPr = paragraph._p.get_or_add_pPr()
    spacing = OxmlElement('w:spacing')
    spacing.set(qn('w:before'), str(before))
    spacing.set(qn('w:after'), str(after))
    if line:

        spacing.set(qn('w:lineRule'), 'auto')
    pPr.append(spacing)

    tc = cell._tc

    shd.set(qn('w:val'), 'clear')
    shd.set(qn('w:color'), 'auto')

    pPr = paragraph._p.get_or_add_pPr()

    bottom.set(qn('w:val'), 'single')
    bottom.set(qn('w:sz'), str(size))
    bottom.set(qn('w:space'), '4')
    bottom.set(qn('w:color'), color_hex)
    pBdr.append(bottom)
    pPr.append(pBdr)

    spacing.set(qn('w:after'), str(after))

    r_subj.font.size = Pt(11)
    r_subj.font.color.rgb = NAVY
    r_subj.font.name = 'Calibri'

    body_paragraphs = [

            "not just because of the job itself, but because of the context around it. You\u2019re working at the "
            "intersection of enterprise software and the insurance domain, embedded within a parent company "
            "(WWK) that genuinely depends on your team to get it right. That\u2019s exactly the kind of environment "
            "I work well in: high stakes, real consequences, and a team that takes quality seriously."
        ),

        (
            "I\u2019ve spent the last eight years building and shipping enterprise software solutions, and throughout "
            "that time quality assurance has never been something I handed off at the end \u2014 it\u2019s been woven "

            "estimating test effort alongside developers and coordinating with business stakeholders to agree on "
            "what \u201cdone\u201d actually meant. That kind of collaboration \u2014 between testers, analysts, and "
            "developers \u2014 is where good testing happens, and it\u2019s something I\u2019m genuinely good at."
        ),

        (
            "On the technical side: I\u2019m comfortable with Python scripting for test automation, I\u2019ve used "

            "\u2014 exactly what I\u2019d expect in a commission and provisioning context."
        ),

        (
            "I\u2019m also in a good position practically: I hold an EU passport, I\u2019m based in Freiburg and "
            "open to relocating to Hamburg, and my German is at a C1 level so I can work, communicate, and review "
            "German-language specifications without it being a bottleneck."
        ),

        (
            "I\u2019d genuinely enjoy a conversation about the role \u2014 both to hear more about the team and "
            "the systems you\u2019re working with, and to give you a better sense of how I approach test design in "

            "start date at that stage."
        ),

        "Thank you for your time, and I look forward to hearing from you.",

        "Best regards,\nDolapo Nuga",

        r.font.name = 'Calibri'
        if i == 0:

            r.font.color.rgb = DARK_NAVY

    doc.save(CL_OUTPUT)
    print(f"Cover letter saved to: {CL_OUTPUT}")
